title: Login redirect loop after SSO change
body:
We recently switched our company's SSO provider from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Since the switch, some users are getting stuck in an infinite redirect loop when trying to log into TaskFlow. They click login, get redirected to Microsoft, authenticate successfully, get redirected back to TaskFlow, and then immediately get sent back to Microsoft again. Not all users are affected — maybe 30% of our team. The rest can log in fine.

We've already verified that the Entra ID app registration is correct and matches our redirect URIs. The affected users can log into other apps that use the same SSO just fine.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — that redirect loop pattern is a classic SSO migration issue and the 30% split is a strong clue.

To start narrowing this down: **what do the affected users have in common that the unaffected users don't?** Specifically:

- Do the affected users have a different email domain, username format, or account type in Entra ID (e.g., guest accounts vs. member accounts, or `user@company.com` vs. `user@subsidiary.com`)?
- Were any of the affected users' accounts migrated differently, or do they have any legacy Okta sessions/cookies they haven't cleared?

Even a hunch here would help — any pattern you've noticed between who's stuck and who's fine.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Good question. So I've been looking at this and I think there might be a pattern with email addresses. A bunch of the affected users use plus addressing in their emails — like jane+taskflow@company.com — which we had set up in Okta. Also, I know at least a couple of the affected people had their email addresses changed or aliased at some point, like they originally signed up with a personal email and then we linked their company email later. The users who are working fine seem to just have straightforward email addresses. They're all member accounts, same domain, nothing weird there. I did tell everyone to clear their cookies when we switched over, but I'm not 100% sure everyone actually did that. The ones I've personally helped clear cookies still have the issue though.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That plus-addressing pattern is a very strong lead — this is almost certainly a claim-mismatch issue between what Entra ID sends back and what TaskFlow has stored for those users.

To confirm: **can you check TaskFlow's server-side auth logs during a failed login attempt from an affected user?** Specifically, I need to know:

1. What identity value is Entra ID sending back in the token? (Look for the `email`, `preferred_username`, or `upn` claim in the OIDC token or SAML assertion.) For example, is it returning `jane@company.com` when TaskFlow has `jane+taskflow@company.com` stored?
2. Is TaskFlow logging any kind of "user not found" or session-creation failure, or does it just silently redirect?

If you don't have easy access to the auth logs, an alternative: could you look up an affected user in TaskFlow's database and tell me what email/identifier is stored for them, and then compare it to what Entra ID shows as that user's primary email or UPN?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
I'm not super familiar with digging into OIDC token claims directly, but I did pull up the TaskFlow admin panel and compared a couple accounts. For one affected user, TaskFlow has her stored as jane+taskflow@company.com, but in Entra ID her primary email is just jane@company.com — so yeah, that lines up with what you're saying. The plus part is getting stripped or something.

As for logs, I checked the TaskFlow logs briefly and I don't see an obvious "user not found" error, it's more like it just keeps redirecting. One thing I did notice when I had an affected user try logging in with the browser dev tools open — there's a Set-Cookie header in the redirect response coming back from Microsoft, but it seems like the cookie isn't actually sticking? I'm not sure if that's related or just a red herring. The cookie thing happens even for the users with the email mismatch issue, so maybe it's all the same problem.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"SSO redirect loop for users with plus-addressed or aliased emails after Okta-to-Entra-ID migration","problem":"After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop during login. They authenticate successfully with Entra ID but TaskFlow cannot match them to an existing account, fails to establish a session, and redirects them back to the IdP indefinitely.","root_cause_hypothesis":"Identity claim mismatch between Entra ID and TaskFlow's user store. Okta preserved plus-addressed emails (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) in its claims, so TaskFlow stored those as the canonical user identifier. Entra ID normalizes or strips plus-addressing, returning the base email (jane@company.com) in the email/UPN claim. TaskFlow's auth callback performs a user lookup against the stored email, finds no match, fails to create a session, and falls through to re-initiating the auth flow — producing the redirect loop. The same mechanism likely affects users whose emails were aliased or changed, if the stored identifier no longer matches what Entra ID returns.","reproduction_steps":["Identify a user whose TaskFlow account email uses plus-addressing (e.g., user+taskflow@company.com) or was previously aliased","Have that user attempt to log in via SSO","Observe: successful Entra ID authentication, redirect back to TaskFlow, immediate redirect back to Entra ID, loop continues","Compare the email claim in the Entra ID token (visible in browser dev tools network tab, or server auth logs) to the email stored in TaskFlow's user record — they will not match"],"environment":"TaskFlow instance recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. All users are member accounts on the same domain. Entra ID app registration and redirect URIs have been verified as correct. Affected users can authenticate to other Entra ID apps without issue.","severity":"high","impact":"Approximately 30% of the team is completely locked out of TaskFlow. No workaround exists for affected users short of database or configuration changes. This is a total login failure, not a degraded experience.","recommended_fix":"Two-part fix: (1) **Immediate remediation:** Update the stored email/identifier for affected users in TaskFlow's database to match what Entra ID returns (the base email without plus-addressing). This unblocks users now. (2) **Auth code hardening:** Modify TaskFlow's OIDC callback user-lookup logic to normalize email comparisons (e.g., strip plus-addressing before lookup, or match on a stable claim like `sub` or `oid` rather than email). Also add a fallback: if user lookup fails after successful IdP authentication, log a clear error and display a login failure page instead of silently re-initiating the auth flow — this prevents redirect loops for any future identity mismatches.","proposed_test_case":"Create a test user in TaskFlow with a plus-addressed email (test+tag@company.com). Configure the test IdP to return the base email (test@company.com) in the email claim. Attempt login and verify: (a) the user is matched and authenticated successfully after the fix, and (b) if matching still fails for any reason, a clear error page is shown instead of a redirect loop.","information_gaps":["Exact OIDC claim TaskFlow uses for user lookup (email vs. preferred_username vs. sub) — needs code inspection","Whether the cookie not sticking is purely a symptom of the failed session creation or if there is also a SameSite/Secure/Domain cookie configuration issue introduced during migration","Full list of affected users and whether all of them have the plus-addressing or alias pattern, or if there are outliers with a different cause"]}
